epic.arc: An agent is summoned not as a flat text string but as a governed, composed working environment — prompt-as-code + the prompt's declared edges + the code path that delivers it + a composed environment + observability + auth — and the loop spends these agents as utensils to fill a volume. We define the volume, the expectations, and the rewards and consequences; we do NOT define the model. The arc closes the delta between what the agent-summoning requirements ledger writes down and what is real, then adds the features on top.
epic.context: Captured in .ai-native/proposals/agent-summoning-requirements.md (the requirements ledger, in the shape of administrator-requirements.md). The triggering gap: the tend workflows summoned a subagent with one hand-typed paragraph + an output schema and nothing else — no versioned prompt, no fingerprint, no requirements check, no composed environment, no receipt, no auth. The §7 node rail (prompt-as-code, hash-pinned, rung-checked, receipted) already exists for the node/gate path; the Workflow-tool spawn path went around it with strings. This arc re-founds agent summoning on the written rail and makes every agent run witnessed. bdo's framing, 2026-06-22: run it on the books as a training program, monitored — assert nothing as done, record everything, let the evidence earn the realness stamp.
epic.value: Every agent the loop spends is summoned the written-down way (governed prompt + hash + rung + composed environment) and its run is on the books (a witnessed receipt under a training posture), so no agent runs off-record and the work counts toward the volume. A session that fans out subagents does so on one rail; the owner watches a monitor fold, not a vanishing.
epic.horizon: The Administrator (a session) spends workflows and subagents — all on the rail — to fill a declared volume, each agent prompt-as-code + composed-environment + rung-checked + receipted, every run readable in a monitor fold. The summon becomes a workshop, not a sticky note; the prompt-requirements door and the per-run book are real gates, not conventions.

pieces.glue: The on-the-books + training-posture + monitor layer (C6 + R5), composing the recovered C1 (the tend-heal governed node prompt) and C3 (the prompt-requirements validator). loop/agent_run.py mirrors loop/train.py one axis over: an agent_training_run posture opened on the log, every run booked as an agent_run receipt stamped training:true, fold_run as the monitor read; book_receipt refuses a ghost node or a mismatched prompt hash. tend-heal.js gains Open/Book phases so every workflow agent is witnessed by construction. The first real piece; value-gate accepted (rcp.c21c49ac30c1) after one amend that dropped an unbacked-arc claim and a bundled issue-pen verb. PR #554.

FUTURE (C2 + R7) — a utility that, given a role + section + targets, assembles the agent's working environment (the governed prompt, the relevant CLAUDE.md surfaces, code examples, the workflow diagram, the named pens) into the summon. The summon becomes a workshop, not a sticky note. Not yet built.

FUTURE (C4 + C5 + R8) — bridge the Workflow-tool agent() spawn through the same summon/brand/rung path the §7 node rail uses, carrying a class and a default-deny rung check; an off-rail spawn is surfaced, not silent. One rail for every agent. Not yet built.

FUTURE (C3 + R10) — promote the prompt-requirements validator (loop/prompt_req.py, already built and green) into a gate the rail enforces at summon: a prompt missing role/reads/returns/won't/evals HALTS rather than running ungoverned. Not yet a pipeline gate.

epic.build_order: atom.agent-runs-on-the-books (this PR, the witnessed run) -> spawn-on-rail (every agent governed) -> prompt-requirements-gate (the door becomes a gate) -> environment-composer (the workshop). Each piece lands as a stamped increment with its own done-line, its own §10 teeth, and an independent review before it counts. The Administrator (D3) is the standing form once the rail carries every agent.
epic.provenance: Authored by Claude from .ai-native/proposals/agent-summoning-requirements.md (the captured requirements ledger) and bdo's 2026-06-22 direction ('run it on the books as a training program, monitored'). The first piece (atom.agent-runs-on-the-books.v0) is real and value-gate accepted; the remaining pieces are sketched from the ledger's C/D items and are not yet built. Confirmed by bdo's confirm-arc gesture (his in-session 'Recommended' directive choosing the named-arc route).
